PR #9 opened
Review (block): feat: build-time docs rendering from chalk-protocol markdown (/protocol, /quickstart, /research)
Blocked: feat: build-time docs rendering from chalk-protocol markdown (/protocol, /quickstart, /research) (needs human-input)
description: review blocked: [high/test-adequacy] Criterion 3 explicitly requires a LOCKED test covering all three pages, but the task's locked acceptance tests list is '(none)'. test/docs-pages.test.mjs does cover /protocol, /quickstart, /research, the landing links, and the failed-fetch build failure, but it is not registered as a locked test, so it can be freely weakened or deleted later — the 'locked' clause of the contract is unmet. Register it via the sanctioned path (e.g. chalk amend-spec --test test/docs-pages.test.mjs) before this can pass.; [med/test-adequacy] The render tests only assert 'some h1/h2 exists' and a source link back to GitHub; they never compare page content against the fetched upstream markdown. Criterion 1 ('renders the parent repo's CURRENT markdown') would still 'pass' if the loader silently served stale/cached or partially wrong content, as long as any heading rendered. Only the DOCS_BASE_URL failure test ties output to the fetch path at all.; [med/regression] The failed-fetch test and the sandbox build share the repo's node_modules (symlinked), so Astro's content-layer data store under node_modules/.astro is shared between the root build (test/build.test.mjs) and both sandbox builds. --test-concurrency=1 serializes test FILES, but this relies on undocumented file-ordering and shared-cache behavior; a future removal of that flag (or parallel CI shards) reintroduces the race the comment warns about. Consider an isolated cache dir per build instead of a global concurrency clamp.; [low/design-intent] Generated artifacts (.astro/collections/docs.schema.json, .astro/content.d.ts) are committed as part of the change; these are build outputs Astro regenerates and will churn/drift (content.d.ts already points at src/content.config.js while the source is content.config.ts).; [low/correctness] The heading assertion /<h[12][\s>]/ and the slug.toUpperCase() source-link check depend on upstream markdown shape and the PROTOCOL/QUICKSTART/RESEARCH naming convention; an upstream doc starting at h3 or a renamed file makes the suite fail for reasons unrelated to this site's code. Acceptable for a build-time mirror, but worth noting as fragility, and the happy-path build requires live network access to raw.githubusercontent.com.
